Title: xCatalog - Search Products Query
Preconditions: GraphQL endpoint accessible at BACK_URL, products in catalog
Steps:
1. Navigate to BACK_URL in browser
2. Open GraphQL playground or use browser_evaluate to send query
3. Execute: query { products(storeId: STORE_ID, keyword: "laptop") { totalCount items { id name code imgSrc } } }
4. Verify response
Expected Result:
- Response returns data.products
- totalCount > 0 for valid keyword
- Items contain id, name, code
- No errors in response

Title: xCatalog - Get Product Detail Query
Steps:
1. Execute: query { product(storeId: STORE_ID, id: "PRODUCT_ID") { id name descriptions { content } prices { list { amount } sale { amount } } } }
2. Verify all fields populated
Expected Result:
- Product detail returned
- Descriptions present
- Prices include list and sale amounts
- Images array populated

Title: xCatalog - Category Tree Query
Steps:
1. Execute: query { categories(storeId: STORE_ID) { totalCount items { id name slug childCategories { id name } } } }
2. Verify category hierarchy
Expected Result:
- Root categories returned
- Child categories nested correctly
- Each category has slug for URL
- totalCount matches actual count

Title: xCatalog - Product Facets/Filters Query
Steps:
1. Execute products query with facet parameter
2. Verify aggregations in response
3. Apply filter and re-query
4. Verify filtered results
Expected Result:
- Facets/aggregations returned (brand, price range, etc.)
- Filter narrows results correctly
- facetValues contain count per value
- Combining filters works (AND logic)

Title: xCart - Create Cart Mutation
Steps:
1. Execute: mutation { createCart(storeId: STORE_ID, userId: USER_ID, currencyCode: "USD", cultureName: "en-US") { id } }
2. Verify cart created
Expected Result:
- Cart created with ID
- No errors in response
- Cart retrievable by ID

Title: xCart - Add Item to Cart Mutation
Preconditions: Cart created from GQL-005, product ID known
Steps:
1. Execute: mutation { addItem(cartId: CART_ID, productId: PRODUCT_ID, quantity: 2) { id itemsCount items { id productId quantity listPrice { amount } } } }
2. Verify item added
Expected Result:
- Item added to cart
- itemsCount incremented
- Quantity set to 2
- listPrice populated with correct amount

Title: xCart - Update Cart Item Quantity
Steps:
1. Execute: mutation { changeCartItemQuantity(cartId: CART_ID, lineItemId: LINE_ITEM_ID, quantity: 5) { id items { id quantity } totals { subTotal { amount } } } }
2. Verify quantity updated
Expected Result:
- Quantity changed to 5
- Subtotal recalculated
- No errors

Title: xCart - Remove Item from Cart
Steps:
1. Execute: mutation { removeCartItem(cartId: CART_ID, lineItemId: LINE_ITEM_ID) { id itemsCount items { id } } }
2. Verify item removed
Expected Result:
- Item removed from cart
- itemsCount decremented
- Totals recalculated
- Cart still valid

Title: xCart - Set Shipping Address
Steps:
1. Execute addOrUpdateCartShipment mutation with address
2. Verify address set on cart
3. Query cart to confirm shipping info
Expected Result:
- Shipping address saved
- City, state, zip, country populated
- No validation errors
- Cart ready for shipping method selection

Title: xCart - Apply Coupon Mutation
Preconditions: Cart with items, valid coupon code
Steps:
1. Execute: mutation { addCoupon(cartId: CART_ID, couponCode: "TESTCOUPON") { id coupons { code isAppliedSuccessfully } totals { discountTotal { amount } } } }
2. Verify coupon applied
Expected Result:
- Coupon appears in coupons array
- isAppliedSuccessfully = true
- discountTotal shows discount amount
- Invalid coupon returns appropriate error

Title: xOrder - Get Orders Query
Steps:
1. Execute: query { orders(filter: "", sort: "createdDate:desc") { totalCount items { id number status createdDate total { amount } } } }
2. Verify order list
Expected Result:
- Orders returned sorted by date
- Each order has number, status, total
- totalCount matches
- Pagination works (first/after)

Title: xOrder - Get Order Detail Query
Steps:
1. Execute order query by ID/number
2. Verify full order detail
3. Check line items, addresses, payments
Expected Result:
- Full order detail returned
- Line items with product info
- Shipping address populated
- Payment info present
- Order totals correct

Title: xOrder - Create Order from Cart
Preconditions: Cart with items, shipping, payment configured
Expected Result:
- Order created successfully
- Order number generated
- Status is 'New' or 'Processing'
- Cart cleared after order creation

Title: xCMS - Get Page Content Query
Steps:
1. Execute: query { pages(storeId: STORE_ID) { totalCount items { id name relativeUrl content } } }
2. Verify page content
Expected Result:
- Pages returned
- Content populated
- RelativeUrl set
- No HTML injection in content

Title: GraphQL Error Handling - Invalid Query
Steps:
1. Send malformed query syntax
2. Send query for non-existent field
3. Send mutation with missing required args
Expected Result:
- Syntax error returns clear error message
- Unknown field returns specific field error
- Missing args returns validation error
- No 500 errors, all return structured GraphQL errors

Title: GraphQL Authentication - Unauthenticated Access
Steps:
1. Query products without auth token (should work - public)
2. Query orders without auth token (should fail)
3. Execute cart mutation without auth (should fail)
Expected Result:
- Public queries work without auth
- Protected queries return authentication error
- Mutations require valid token
- Error messages don't leak sensitive info

Title: xCart - Full Checkout Flow via GraphQL
Preconditions: User auth, products available, payment configured
Steps:
1. Create cart
2. Add multiple items
3. Set shipping address
4. Get available shipping methods
5. Set shipping method
6. Set payment method
7. Validate cart
8. Create order
Expected Result:
- Each step succeeds
- Cart totals update correctly at each stage
- Shipping rates calculated
- Order created with all details
- End-to-end flow completes in < 30 seconds

Title: GraphQL Query Performance
Steps:
1. Execute products search query - measure response time
2. Execute complex nested query (product with variants, prices, images)
3. Execute orders list query
4. Check for N+1 query patterns in response times
Expected Result:
- Product search < 500ms
- Nested product query < 1000ms
- Orders list < 500ms
- No obvious N+1 patterns (nested queries don't multiply response time)

Title: xCatalog - Product Variations Query
Steps:
1. Query product with variations
2. Verify variation properties (size, color, etc.)
3. Check availability per variation
4. Verify pricing per variation
Expected Result:
- Variations array populated
- Each variation has distinct properties
- Availability differs per variation (if configured)
- Prices may differ per variation

Title: GraphQL Introspection Security
Expected Result:
- Introspection either disabled (production best practice) or limited
- No internal/debug types exposed
- Schema does not reveal implementation details
- Rate limiting applies to introspection queries

Title: xCart - Cart Uses Live Price After Admin Price Change (Not Cached Index)
Preconditions: Authenticated user, product with known price in price list, access to Admin API at ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Note current price of Product A via xCatalog products query
2. Change Product A price via REST API: PUT ${BACK_URL}/api/pricing/pricelists/{priceListId}/prices with new amount
3. Immediately (within 5 seconds) execute addItem mutation to add Product A to cart
4. Query cart: query { cart(storeId: STORE_ID, cartId: CART_ID) { items { productId listPrice { amount } placedPrice { amount } } } }
5. Compare cart line item placedPrice against NEW price set in step 2
Expected Result:
- Cart line item placedPrice equals the NEW price from step 2 (not the old cached/indexed price)
- listPrice reflects the updated price list value
- No mixed pricing: all price fields on the line item use the live price
- BL-CROSS-002: Cart must use live (current) price even during reindex lag window

Title: xCatalog - Product Listing Price During Reindex Lag Window
Preconditions: Product with known price, Elasticsearch indexing enabled, access to Admin API
Steps:
1. Query xCatalog for Product A and record current listed price: query { products(storeId: STORE_ID, query: "ProductA_code") { items { id name prices { list { amount } } } } }
2. Change Product A price via REST API: PUT ${BACK_URL}/api/pricing/pricelists/{priceListId}/prices
3. Immediately re-query xCatalog products (same query as step 1)
4. Document whether old or new price is returned (reindex lag is acceptable per BL-CROSS-002)
5. Wait 60 seconds for reindex to complete
6. Re-query xCatalog products again
7. Verify price now reflects the updated value
Expected Result:
- Step 3: Old price MAY be returned (acceptable reindex lag of 30-60s per BL-CROSS-002)
- Step 6: After reindex window, xCatalog MUST return the new price
- Document actual lag duration observed
- If price still shows old value after 90 seconds, flag as potential reindex failure
- Note: This is informational/monitoring — storefront listing lag is acceptable but cart price must be live (see GQL-021)

Title: xCart - Price Update Reflected on Re-query After Admin Change
Preconditions: Cart with existing item at known price, access to Admin API
Steps:
1. Create cart and add Product A (quantity 1) via addItem mutation
2. Query cart and record placedPrice for the line item
3. Change Product A price in Admin via REST API: PUT ${BACK_URL}/api/pricing/pricelists/{priceListId}/prices with different amount
4. Re-query the same cart: query { cart(storeId: STORE_ID, cartId: CART_ID) { items { productId placedPrice { amount } listPrice { amount } } totals { subTotal { amount } } } }
5. Compare placedPrice before and after price change
Expected Result:
- placedPrice in step 4 reflects the NEW price (updated in step 3)
- totals.subTotal recalculated with the new price
- No stale/cached price persisted in the cart
- BL-CROSS-002: Cart always uses live price regardless of index state
- If placedPrice still shows old value, this is a P0 violation of BL-CROSS-002

Title: xOrder - createOrderFromCart Idempotency (Duplicate Call Returns Same Order)
Preconditions: Full cart ready for order: items added, shipping address set, shipping method selected, payment method configured
Expected Result:
- Step 2: First call succeeds, returns order ID and number
- Step 3: Second call either returns the SAME order ID/number or returns an error indicating cart already converted (NOT a 500 error)
- Step 4: Exactly 1 order created (not 2)
- No duplicate payment charges
- No duplicate inventory decrements
- BL-CROSS-010: All checkout mutations must be idempotent

Title: xOrder - Second createOrderFromCart Returns Same Order ID (Not Server Error)
Preconditions: Full cart ready for order creation
Steps:
1. Prepare cart through full checkout flow
2. Execute createOrderFromCart mutation — record orderId and orderNumber
3. Wait 2 seconds
4. Execute createOrderFromCart with the same cartId again
5. Inspect the response structure of the second call
6. If successful response: verify orderId matches step 2
7. If error response: verify it is a structured GraphQL error (not HTTP 500)
Expected Result:
- Second call MUST NOT return HTTP 500 or unhandled exception
- Acceptable responses: (a) same order ID returned, or (b) structured error like "Cart already converted to order" or "Cart not found"
- response.errors[] if present must have clear message, not stack trace
- No partial order created by second call
- BL-CROSS-010: Idempotent behavior — same input produces same output without side effects

Title: xCart - Cart Invalidated After Order Creation (No Reuse)
Preconditions: Cart converted to order via createOrderFromCart
Expected Result:
- Step 3: addItem returns error (cart not found, cart already converted, or similar)
- Step 4: Cart query returns empty/null or cart with status indicating it is no longer active
- Step 5: Mutation fails with appropriate error
- No silent success that would allow modifying a completed order's source cart
- BL-CROSS-010: Post-order cart must be invalidated to prevent reuse and duplicate orders

Title: xCart - Currency Switch Updates All Line Items to New Price List
Preconditions: Products with prices in both USD and EUR price lists, authenticated user
Expected Result:
- All items[].listPrice.currencyCode = "EUR"
- All items[].placedPrice.currencyCode = "EUR"
- totals.subTotal.currencyCode = "EUR"
- Price amounts correspond to EUR price list values (NOT USD-to-EUR conversion)
- No mixed currency state: zero fields still showing USD
- BL-CROSS-004: Currency switch must update ALL monetary fields atomically

Title: xCart - Shipping Rates Recalculate After Currency Switch
Preconditions: Cart with items and shipping address set, shipping methods configured for both USD and EUR
Steps:
1. Create cart in USD, add items, set shipping address via addOrUpdateCartShipment
2. Query available shipping methods: query { cart(storeId: STORE_ID, cartId: CART_ID) { availableShippingMethods { code optionName price { amount currencyCode } } } }
3. Record shipping rates in USD
4. Switch cart currency to EUR via changeCartCurrency mutation
5. Re-query available shipping methods (same query as step 2)
6. Compare shipping rates before and after currency switch
Expected Result:
- Step 5: All shipping method prices show currencyCode = "EUR"
- Shipping rates are EUR-specific values (not converted from USD)
- No shipping methods still displaying USD amounts
- If a shipping method is unavailable in EUR, it should be excluded from the list
- Cart totals (including shipping) all in EUR
- BL-CROSS-004: Shipping rates must update as part of atomic currency switch

Title: xCart - Product Without New Currency Price Flagged After Currency Switch
Preconditions: Cart with items: Product A has both USD and EUR prices, Product B has ONLY USD price
Steps:
1. Create cart in USD, add Product A and Product B
2. Query cart — both items present with USD prices
3. Switch currency to EUR via changeCartCurrency mutation
4. Query cart: query { cart(storeId: STORE_ID, cartId: CART_ID) { items { productId name listPrice { amount currencyCode } isValid validationErrors { errorCode errorMessage } } validationErrors { errorCode } itemsCount } }
5. Check Product B status (the one without EUR price)
Expected Result:
- Product A: listPrice in EUR, valid, no errors
- Product B: either (a) removed from cart with itemsCount decremented, or (b) flagged with validationErrors indicating no price available in EUR, or (c) isValid = false
- Product B must NOT show a $0.00 EUR price or a converted-from-USD price
- Cart totals must exclude Product B if it has no valid EUR price
- BL-CROSS-004 + BL-PRICE-005: Products without price list in new currency become unavailable
